medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Mr. ___ is a ___ with a history of hypertension, hyperlipidemia, diabetes mellitus, and 2nd degree AVB requiring Bi-V pacemaker placement in ___. He presents with left sided arm pain and swelling since yesterday. He noticed some mild swelling of the left upper extremity while showering yesterday morning, accompanied by a vague sense of numbness described as if his arm "fell asleep." He carried on as usual, but noted this morning that the arm swelling had worsened with a generally redder appearance of the distal extremity. He also had a fullness in the left neck that was mildly tender to touch. These symptoms prompted PCP visit and he was subsequently referred to the emergency department. In the ED, his initial vitals were T98.1, HR90, BP131/94, RR18, O2 sat 100% on room air. Left upper extremity ultrasound showed left axillary DVT. He was started on heparin drip.

physical_examination.general_examination: Admission Exam: VS - Temp 98.3F, BP162/90, HR60, R20, O2-sat 98% RA. GENERAL - well-appearing man in NAD, comfortable, appropriate. NECK - asymmetric fullness of the left supraclavicular area compared to the right, mildly tender to touch. EXTREMITIES - Asymmetric edema of the left upper extremity compared to right with slight increase plethora. Distal pulses are 2+ in all extremities.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: NEURO - awake, A&Ox3, CNs II-XII grossly intact, muscle strength ___ throughout, sensation grossly intact throughout, DTRs 2+ and symmetric, cerebellar exam intact, steady gait.
